frame.question: Should agent-native structures coexist with human prose (dual-layer) or replace prose (migration)?

canonical_precedents: Translation Decision Map.md: public/private boundary principle

A_dual_layer.description: Prose canonical; JSON/YAML as agent cache
A_dual_layer.rejected: Drift risk too high given Taiwan metric staleness precedent

B_migration.rejected: Human readability non-negotiable for public academic package

C_hybrid.description: YAML frontmatter for status/dependencies; prose for reasoning

red_team_challenges.challenge: Problem framing assumes agents need special treatment; future agents will have larger context
red_team_challenges.response: Sweller cognitive load research shows scoped attention outperforms regardless of capacity
red_team_challenges.testable_prediction: If dual-layer shows no validation improvement in 3 months, revert to prose-only

red_team_challenges.challenge: YAML introduces syntax errors prose doesn't have
red_team_challenges.response: Schema validation catches structural errors deterministically
red_team_challenges.testable_prediction: Compare validation failure rates: YAML parsing vs prose staleness

red_team_challenges.challenge: Second control layer creates drift between agent (YAML) and human (prose) views
red_team_challenges.response: Tools check last_synced against mtime; prose is always downstream of YAML

decision.reasoning: Boundary preservation: Research/ stays human-readable; agent constraints handled via scoped .handoff/ packets; federation: subsystems decide representation; validation: tools check YAML, humans read prose
implementation_rules: YAML frontmatter on status/execution-order files only
Tools validate last_synced dates against file mtimes
No prose replacement without explicit migration decision

revisable_when: Validation failure rate not improved at 3-month review
Human readability complaints from academic reviewers
Agent capability improvements eliminate context constraints
success_metrics: Agent navigation time to active task < 2 minutes
No YAML/prose divergence detected in validation
